home.heroHeadline: I turn biology into ventures. And science into health.
home.heroSubtitle: Full Professor at San Raffaele University, Rome, and founder and director of the Human Longevity Program at IRCCS San Raffaele. Top 1% of the world's most influential scientists (Stanford, 2023).

hero.headline: I turn biology into ventures. And science into health.

home.tedxQuote: To beat cancer, you have to think like a cell.

pillars.title: Three pillars, one trajectory
pillars.subtitle: Nanomedicine, regenerative materials and longevity medicine form one translational path: from biological insight to patient impact.

nano.title: Nanomedicine
nano.body: Engineering delivery systems that cross biological barriers and bring therapeutics precisely where disease begins.

regen.title: Regenerative Materials
regen.body: Designing biomimetic scaffolds and extracellular matrices that guide tissue repair through immune-aware regeneration.

science.label: Science
science.desc: Frontier research in nanomedicine, biomimetic materials and longevity medicine — published in Nature Materials, Nature Nanotechnology, and 200+ peer-reviewed journals.

ventures.label: Ventures
ventures.desc: From lab to market: I have founded and led biomedical and biotech initiatives, serving as scientific consultant to Roche, Novartis, Gilead and other global leaders.

longevity.label: Longevity
longevity.desc: Founder and director of the Human Longevity Program at IRCCS San Raffaele, Rome: translational research to extend health span, not just lifespan.
longevity.title: Longevity Medicine
longevity.body: Building clinical and scientific models that extend health span through prevention, precision diagnostics and systems medicine.

leukosome.title: Leukosome
leukosome.body: A biomimetic vesicle that uses leukocyte membrane proteins to navigate inflammation and deliver drugs with precision.

msv.title: Multistage Vector
msv.body: A multistage nanocarrier designed to overcome biological barriers sequentially, from circulation to diseased tissue.

longevity.title: Precision longevity medicine
longevity.body: A preventive medicine model integrating biomarkers, inflammation, metabolism and organ function across the lifespan.

tedx.title: To beat cancer, you have to think like a cell
tedx.body: A talk on how nanomedicine and biomimicry can help us rethink cancer therapy from the point of view of the cell.
tedx.thumbnailAlt: TEDx talk thumbnail featuring Prof. Ennio Tasciotti

about.title: About
about.subtitle: Scientist, entrepreneur and professor bridging basic research and applied medicine.
about.metaDesc: Full Professor at San Raffaele University, Rome, and founder and director of the Human Longevity Program at IRCCS San Raffaele. A career spanning Pisa, Houston and Rome.

bio.title: Biography
bio.p1: I am Ennio Tasciotti. I have spent twenty years building bridges between basic research and applied medicine — from Scuola Normale Superiore di Pisa to the Houston Methodist system, and now to IRCCS San Raffaele and San Raffaele University in Rome.
bio.p2: My work sits at the intersection of nanomedicine, biomimetic materials and longevity medicine. I have directed research centers and programs and authored over 200 peer-reviewed publications.
bio.p3: Alongside research, I have co-founded multiple startups and sit on the scientific advisory boards of pharmaceutical and biotech companies globally.

items.role: Full Professor, Applied Medical Sciences and Technologies
Founder and Director, Human Longevity Program
Founder and Director, Center for Biomimetic Medicine

items.place: Scuola Normale Superiore di Pisa and ICGEB

teaching.title: Teaching
teaching.body: Full Professor of Applied Medical Sciences and Technologies at San Raffaele University, Rome. The course covers innovations in preventive and diagnostic medicine, wellbeing, healthy longevity and rehabilitative medicine. According to the official CV, he has mentored 20 PhD students and 24 master's students.

about.bioIntro: I am Ennio Tasciotti. I have spent twenty years building bridges between basic research and applied medicine — from Scuola Normale Superiore di Pisa to the Houston Methodist system, and now to IRCCS San Raffaele and San Raffaele University in Rome.

science.title: Science
science.subtitle: Nanomedicine, biomimetic materials and longevity medicine — three research areas, 200+ publications, 15 international patents.
science.metaDesc: Nanomedicine, biomimetic materials, longevity medicine: three research areas, 200+ publications, 15 international patents.
science.heroManifesto: My research has merged engineering, biology and medicine to overcome biological barriers in cancer, inflammatory disease and tissue regeneration.

nano.title: Nanomedicine
nano.body: Nanoscale delivery systems engineered to bypass biological barriers and deliver therapeutics with precision into diseased tissue. The Multistage Vector (Nature Nanotechnology, 2008) and the Leukosome (Nature Materials, 2016) are among the most cited outcomes.

regen.title: Biomimetic Materials
regen.body: Extracellular matrix scaffolds and engineered materials that guide bone, cartilage and cardiac tissue regeneration by modulating local immune response without toxicity.

longevity.title: Longevity Medicine
longevity.body: Study of the molecular mechanisms of aging, chronic inflammation and organ function loss. Clinical direction of the Human Longevity Program at IRCCS San Raffaele.
longevity.name: Longevity Medicine
longevity.desc: Study of the molecular mechanisms of aging, chronic inflammation and organ function loss. Clinical direction of the Human Longevity Program at IRCCS San Raffaele.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.metaDesc: Full statement on personal data processing in compliance with GDPR.
privacy.intro: This site is operated by Prof. Ennio Tasciotti. We are committed to protecting your personal data in compliance with GDPR (EU) 2016/679.

controller.title: Data Controller
controller.body: Prof. Ennio Tasciotti, IRCCS San Raffaele, Rome, Italy. San Raffaele University, Rome. Email: ennio.tasciotti@sanraffaele.it

data.title: Data We Collect
data.body: We collect only the data you voluntarily provide via the contact or newsletter forms (name, email, message). We do not sell your data. Data is processed via Resend for transactional emails only.

rights.title: Your Rights
rights.body: Under GDPR you have the right to access, rectify, erase, restrict or object to processing of your personal data, and the right to data portability.

cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.metaDesc: Statement on the use of cookies on enniotasciotti.com.
cookies.intro: This website uses cookies to improve your experience and for analytics purposes.

whatAre.title: What Are Cookies
whatAre.body: Cookies are small text files stored on your device by your browser. They help us understand how visitors use the site.

types.title: Types of Cookies
types.necessary: Necessary cookies: essential for the site to function correctly (e.g. language preference, theme).
types.analytics: Analytics cookies: optional, used to understand site traffic via Google Analytics (with your consent).

manage.title: Managing Cookies
manage.body: You can withdraw consent at any time by clicking the cookie settings button in the site footer. You can also disable cookies via your browser settings.

banner.body: This site uses analytics cookies (Google Analytics). By clicking Accept you consent to their use.
